query: role of endothelin-1 in hemodynamics during hemodialysis

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelin -- Pathophysiology -- Pathophysiologic activity in the Kidney:
retrieved_docs.content: Renal endothelium produces endothelin, which regulates sodium and water excretion. In a condition of volume overload, the shear stress on the endothelium leads to the expression of ET-1 that acts on the thick ascending limb and collecting duct to reduce water and sodium reabsorption mediated with the action of intermediates like nitric oxide and other pathways. [22] This idea was proven in experiments utilizing ETA blockers that showed natriuresis by its effect on the nephron. [23] Renal ET-1 also stimulates the release of angiotensin-II that, in turn, cyclically increases the expression of ET-1. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Hemodialysis -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Dialysis patients develop arterial sclerosis and secondary stiffening that commonly causes isolated systolic hypertension with raised pulse pressure. Intradialytic hypertension occurs in more chronically volume-overloaded patients with intradialytic vascular resistance surges attributable to acute changes in endothelial cell function during dialysis. Reducing dialysate sodium and including poorly dialyzed antihypertensives like carvedilol in the prescription cause improvement. [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelin -- Pathophysiology -- Hypertensive Nephropathy:
retrieved_docs.content: Hypertension leads to increased shear stress and ET-1 production as a cause or effect. Over time, the damage to the kidney ensues and leads to proteinuria. Using ET blockers have shown to be nephroprotective and demonstrated a gradual reduction in glomerulosclerosis and albuminuria along with an increased blood flow with an improved microvessel structure in the cortical kidney. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelin -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: ET-1 is a peptide hormone that is growing significantly due to the detection of involvement in multiple disease processes and its rising trend with age. [27] Other factors found in association with elevated ET-1 were hyperthyroidism, and the tendency to develop atrial fibrillation. Much research takes place on the effects of ET-1 receptor antagonists, which research has gradually revealed to produce a reversal of some diseases such as diabetic and hypertensive nephropathy, to quote a few examples. Drugs such as bosentan and sitaxentan have been life-saving in conditions of pulmonary hypertension, especially among females and other diseases mentioned above. With many of the trials conducted on drugs that act against the peptide, such as for reduction of stroke or subarachnoid hemorrhage with patients on endothelin receptor blockers, it also identifies increasing complications associated with these drugs. [28] One of the significant breakthroughs is its impact on retarding cancer progression. With the pattern of its association with the diseases, ET-1 could likely serve as a marker of cardiovascular stress accounting for endothelial dysfunction. There is yet to understand all of its functions on how it chains itself into many disease processes.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelin -- Pathophysiology -- Systemic Hypertension (HTN)
retrieved_docs.content: Essential HTN has normal levels of ET-1; however, there is a local rise in the ET-1 levels in the vascular walls. ETA activation demonstrates an increase in blood pressure, and ETB activity ameliorates hypertension. Thus, the use of blockers of ET receptors has shown an overall reduction of blood pressure. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelin -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Endothelin is a 21-amino acid long peptide that is a vasoconstrictor produced from endothelial cells, vascular smooth muscle cells (VSMC), macrophages, and the renal medulla. They are known to produce endothelin-1 (ET-1) that acts on the receptors ETA and ETB. These receptors are G-protein coupled cell-surface receptors, and ETA is mainly present on smooth muscle cells. ETB receptors appear on endothelial and renal epithelial cells. Both are present in the lungs. ET-1 is known as a potent vasoconstrictor with proliferative, pro-fibrotic, pro-oxidative, pro-inflammatory properties, and maintenance of the tone of VSMC. Other ETs discovered include ET-2 and ET-3. Endothelins are involved in neurovascular uncoupling, affects cognitive functions and blood pressure. Its influence of blood pressure is an essential part of diseases such as postmenopausal hypertension, pre-eclampsia, and pulmonary hypertension in which it plays a major role in its pathogenesis and hence is a vital cornerstone for appropriate management. [1] [2] [3] In this review, we cover physiology, biosynthesis, and pathophysiology of various diseases that involve endothelin.

retrieved_docs.title: Hemodialysis -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) patients needing dialysis have a build-up of uremic retention solutes, like asymmetric dimethylarginine (ADMA), indoxyl sulfate, and p-cresol, that could have proatherogenic properties. Failing kidneys produce lower amounts of cardiovascular disease and atherogenesis inhibitors like renalase, a soluble monoamine oxidase that regulates blood pressure. In addition, dialysis patients experience a state of chronic inflammation. Biomarkers like CRP, fibrinogen, pentraxin 3 (PTX3), interleukin 6 (IL-6), white blood cell count, and the presence of endothelial cells in circulation independently predict mortality in CKD patients as they indicate endothelial dysfunction.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelial Derived Relaxation Factor -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Type 1 endothelial activation, type 2 endothelial activation, endothelial apoptosis, and endothelial necrosis, all lead to endothelial dysfunction. Type 1 endothelial activation occurs almost immediately and does not require gene transcription or de novo protein synthesis. Type 2 endothelial activation can occur over a longer period, taking hours, days, or even months in some cases. In contrast to type 1 endothelial dysfunction, type 2 endothelial dysfunction requires gene transcription and de novo protein synthesis, thus requiring a longer period to progress. If endothelial activation is uncontrolled, type 2 endothelial activation can lead to endothelial apoptosis. Chronic endothelial activation can then lead to endothelial necrosis.  This is significant in cardiorenal syndrome, where endothelial dysfunction may serve as an intersection between renal dysfunction and cardiac dysfunction. [8]

retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Dialysis Fistula -- Anatomy and Physiology
retrieved_docs.content: An arteriovenous fistula is a surgical connection between an artery and a vein. Dialysis fistula maturation is a dynamic process called venous arterialization in which structural and functional remodeling of the venous wall is facilitated by the release of nitrous oxide and the breakdown of elastin to permit enlargement of the outflow vein. [7] Clinically mature arteriovenous fistulas are easier to cannulate with repetitive needlesticks and provide high flow rates necessary to sustain hemodialysis.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelin -- Pathophysiology -- Pathophysiologic Basis of Diseases
retrieved_docs.content: Endothelin functions in an autocrine and/or paracrine fashion. It virtually affects almost all organs of the body. The most important diseases to know, which have established associations with endothelin as a part of their pathophysiology are pulmonary hypertension, heart failure, post-menopausal hypertension, pre-eclampsia, ovarian cancer, and chronic kidney diseases. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Hemodialysis -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Cardiovascular pathologies in CKD range from generalized vasculopathy, vascular noncompliance, and calcification to left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH). The diseases that closely resemble congestive heart failure and fluid overload should be ruled out—for example, acute respiratory distress syndrome, bacterial pneumonia, cirrhosis, community-acquired pneumonia, and pulmonary fibrosis. Non-cardiogenic causes of fluid overload often lack jugular venous distention and an S3 gallop. Measuring left ventricular pressure (LVP) through the pulmonary capillary wedge pressure (PCWP) helps distinguish between cardiogenic and non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema. PCWP is almost always low in those with non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema. Other conditions considered among the differentials are pulmonary embolism, angina, coronary artery disease, aortic dissection, structural heart disease, hyperthyroidism, stress, and excess caffeine and nicotine, besides esophageal disorders like esophagitis, esophageal rupture, and esophageal spasm. The concurrent decrease in the functioning of both kidneys and the heart makes dialysis a critical intervention for preventing and treating life-threatening cardiac conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: Diabetic Nephropathy -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Hyperfiltration is also mediated by vascular regulation. Prostaglandins and atrial natriuretic peptides are 2 potential mediators that reduce arteriolar resistance, further contributing to hyperfiltration. Both are elevated in patients with diabetic nephropathy, particularly those with severe albuminuria (>3.0 g/d). [27] [30] Endothelial dysfunction is another factor linked to glomerular hyperfiltration, with increased endothelin-1 levels observed in patients with T2D and proteinuria. Although endothelin receptor blockers have not shown efficacy to date, ongoing research continues in this area. [27] [31]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Mean Arterial Pressure -- Mechanism
retrieved_docs.content: The renal system helps to maintain MAP primarily through the regulation of plasma volume, which directly affects the cardiac output. A drop in renal perfusion triggers the release of renin, launching the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone cascade. Aldosterone acts on the distal convoluted renal tubules to increase sodium reabsorption and therefore increase water reuptake and plasma volume. Angiotensin II acts on the vasculature via the AT1 receptor to induce smooth muscle contraction, resulting in vasoconstriction. The AT1 receptor is Gq-coupled and works via the same mechanism as the ET-1 and alpha1 receptors mentioned above. Together these changes will increase both cardiac output and systemic vascular resistance to increase MAP.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelin -- Pathophysiology -- Diabetic Nephropathy (DN):
retrieved_docs.content: Hyperglycemia is an inducer for ET-1 production, which causes disassembly of the actin cytoskeleton in diabetic nephropathy. ET antagonists have proven to reduce proteinuria in patients having diabetic nephropathy and reversing the disassembly to an extent. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Contrast-Induced Nephropathy -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: The vasodilator - nitric oxide (NO) The vasoconstrictors - vasopressin, adenosine, angiotensin 2, and endothelins

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 2 -- Community 1080
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis is a renal replacement therapy that can precipitate several acute and chronic complications. Acute disorder of hemodialysis arises as a direct consequence of hemodialysis and typically follows a sudden‑onset clinical course. Likewise, first use syndrome of dialysis, also caused by hemodialysis, presents with a sudden‑onset clinical course and is synonymous with dialysis disequilibrium syndrome (متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى). Hemodialysis is clinically associated with chronic hypertension, disorders of kidney function (اختلال وظيفة الكلى), microcytic anemia (فقر دم صغير الكريات), and acute renal failure (قصور كلوي حاد). In addition, hemodialysis may be linked to low blood pressure (ضغط الدم المنخفض), as reflected in its SSC (systemic side‑effect) relationship. The concept of sudden onset serves as a contextual qualifier for the clinical courses of several conditions, including hypertension with complications and secondary hypertension, dialysis disequilibrium syndrome, and sudden acquired retinal degeneration. Hypertension with complications and secondary hypertension is interpreted through blood pressure measurements and is clinically similar to malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis. Blood pressure itself is related to low blood pressure, and it is a key component of comprehensive cardiovascular evaluation protocols such as tilt‑table testing with continuous ECG and intermittent blood pressure monitoring. Together, these interconnections highlight that hemodialysis can trigger acute syndromes with rapid onset, exacerbate chronic hypertensive states, and affect hematologic and renal parameters, underscoring the importance of vigilant blood pressure monitoring and cardiovascular assessment in patients undergoing dialysis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2842
retrieved_docs.content: Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis and essential hypertension are clinically similar conditions, as both involve pathological changes in small arterial vessels that lead to elevated blood pressure and related end‑organ effects. Recognizing the overlap in their clinical presentations helps clinicians differentiate primary hypertension from secondary renal vascular disease, guides appropriate diagnostic evaluation, and informs management strategies aimed at controlling blood pressure and preserving kidney function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 1961
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis is linked to a spectrum of acute and chronic complications. Acute disorder of hemodialysis and First use syndrome of dialysis both arise as direct consequences of hemodialysis and are characterized by a sudden‑onset clinical course. The dialysis‑related condition described as متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى (dialysis‑syndrome) is also caused by hemodialysis and shares this abrupt clinical presentation. Clinically, hemodialysis is associated with chronic hypertension, اختلال وظيفة الكلى (renal functional impairment), فقر دم صغير الكريات (microcytic anemia), and acute renal failure (قصور كلوي حاد). Hemodialysis can also precipitate low blood pressure (ضغط الدم المنخفض). In addition, Sudden acquired retinal degeneration is noted to have a sudden‑onset clinical course, illustrating that abrupt onset is a common qualifier for several severe medical events. These interrelationships underscore the importance of vigilant monitoring for both systemic and organ‑specific adverse effects in patients receiving hemodialysis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 205
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis is a specific modality of غسيل الكلى (dialysis) that may be recorded as غسيل الكلى غير محدد (unspecified dialysis) and is performed through the creation of an external arteriovenous shunt (Arteriovenous shunt for renal dialysis by external cannula) classified under “Creation of external arteriovenous shunt.” In clinical practice hemodialysis is frequently associated with chronic hypertension (hypertension chronic), اليوريمية (uremia), and فقر دم صغير الكريات (microcytic anemia). Acute complications of hemodialysis include Acute disorder of hemodialysis, First use syndrome of dialysis, متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى (dialysis disequilibrium syndrome), acute renal failure (قصور كلوي حاد) and membership in the acute kidney failure syndrome (الفشل الكلوي الحاد (SMQ)). Hemodialysis also participates in the drug‑interaction syndrome with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (متلازمة التفاعل الدوائي المترافق مع كثرة اليوزينيات والأعراض الجهازية (SMQ)). Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 1 -- Community 78
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis (unspecified dialysis) and other procedures on the urinary system are closely linked to chronic hypertension, kidney function disorder, micro‑cytic anemia, low blood pressure, acute renal failure, acute kidney failure, and chronic kidney disease (CKD). Hemodialysis predisposes to dialysis‑dysfunction syndrome, drug‑interaction syndrome with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms, first‑use syndrome of dialysis, acute disorder of hemodialysis, hypertension with complications, secondary hypertension, acute blood‑loss anemia, and sudden‑acquired retinal degeneration. Benign and malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis represent kidney vascular disorders that are clinically analogous to essential (primary) hypertension, diastolic hypertension, systolic hypertension, hypertensive crisis, and secondary hypertension, and they contribute to kidney dysfunction, primary pulmonary hypertension, depressive disorders, tremor, aortic, peripheral and visceral artery aneurysms, arterial

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1963
retrieved_docs.content: The arteriovenous shunt for renal dialysis by external cannula is a vascular access device whose primary focus is غسيل الكلى, the process of renal dialysis. This shunt provides a reliable conduit for blood to flow from the patient to the dialysis machine and back, enabling effective removal of waste products and excess fluid in individuals with kidney failure. By facilitating efficient extracorporeal circulation, the external cannula arteriovenous shunt plays a central role in the delivery of غسيل الكلى and supports optimal patient care during chronic dialysis therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2554
retrieved_docs.content: Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis is classified as a subtype of اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية (renal vascular disorder) and shares clinical characteristics with essential hypertension, indicating that patients with this form of arteriolar kidney disease often present with blood‑pressure patterns and target‑organ effects similar to those seen in essential hypertension. Recognizing this relationship helps clinicians differentiate primary hypertensive disease from secondary renal vascular pathology and guides appropriate diagnostic evaluation and management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2856
retrieved_docs.content: Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis and essential hypertension are clinically similar conditions, meaning that the renal changes characteristic of benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis often accompany or mimic the hemodynamic abnormalities seen in essential hypertension. In medical terminology, essential hypertension is interpreted by the Arabic phrase “ضغط الدم,” reflecting its cross‑linguistic identification. Recognizing the clinical similarity between benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis and essential hypertension helps clinicians understand that patients with essential hypertension may develop benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis, and that renal findings suggestive of benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis may indicate underlying essential hypertension, thereby guiding comprehensive assessment and management of blood‑pressure‑related kidney disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2863
retrieved_docs.content: Malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis is clinically similar to hypertension with complications and secondary hypertension, indicating that the severe renal vascular changes seen in malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis share comparable clinical manifestations, risk factors, and disease mechanisms with complicated primary hypertension and hypertension that arises secondary to other underlying conditions. This similarity underscores the importance of recognizing overlapping signs and symptoms when diagnosing and managing patients with advanced hypertensive organ damage and secondary causes of elevated blood pressure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 2 -- Community 921
retrieved_docs.content: Essential hypertension is identified and interpreted through increased blood pressure measurements, which are central to its clinical assessment. Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis exhibits clinical features that are similar to those of essential hypertension, highlighting overlapping manifestations and the need to consider both conditions when evaluating patients with elevated blood pressure and related renal changes.

retrieved_docs.content: الكلوي الحاد (SMQ)), reinforcing the connection between inflammatory renal disorders and acute renal insufficiency. In the context of renal replacement therapy, arteriovenous shunts created with external cannulas are specifically focused on facilitating hemodialysis, highlighting the procedural infrastructure required for effective treatment. Finally, hemodialysis is recognized as a member of the Medical & Physical Treatment subcluster, underscoring its central role in therapeutic strategies for renal disease and its integration with related clinical entities and management approaches.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 184
retrieved_docs.content: Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis, a subtype of اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية (renal vascular disorder), is clinically similar to essential hypertension. Essential hypertension includes the concept of ارتفاع ضغط الدم (SMQ) and is interpreted through measurement of ضغط الدم (blood pressure). ضغط الدم is also a central component of the comprehensive assessment titled Evaluation of cardiovascular function with tilt table evaluation, with continuous ECG monitoring and intermittent blood pressure monitoring, with or without pharmacological intervention. ضغط الدم has a relationship with ضغط الدم المنخفض, reflecting the clinical continuum from hypertension to hypotension. The Vascular System is the anatomic site associated with both اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية and تسلخ الشرايين (arterial ulceration). Additionally, ارتفاع ضغط الدم (SMQ) is listed as a member of جراحة إعادة التوعي الكلوي (renal revascularization surgery), indicating that high blood pressure may be addressed by this surgical intervention. Together, these connections illustrate the intertwined nature of renal vascular pathology, systemic blood‑pressure regulation, diagnostic evaluation, and therapeutic strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1786
retrieved_docs.content: Values that are “Above reference range” are considered “Increased.” An “Increased” measurement is the typical interpretation of “essential hypertension,” reflecting elevated blood pressure. “Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis” is clinically similar to “essential hypertension” and is categorized under the parent condition “اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية” (renal vascular disorder). Both “اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية” and “تسلخ الشرايين” (arterial ulceration) have the “Vascular System” listed as their associated anatomic site, highlighting the central role of the vascular system in these renal and arterial pathologies. Recognizing the connections among elevated values, essential hypertension, benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis, and their shared vascular involvement aids clinicians in comprehensive assessment and management of patients with high‑blood‑pressure–related vascular disorders.

Collectively, these interrelated entities illustrate a structured network in which dialysis‑related arteriovenous shunt creation, diverse catheter interventions, coronary therapeutic techniques, hemodynamic monitoring, and extremity arterial surgeries are integrated under cardiovascular surgical and procedural frameworks, emphasizing their coordinated role in the diagnosis, treatment, and management of vascular disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 1467
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis, which is a specific modality under the broader category of غسيل الكلى (renal dialysis), is clinically associated with chronic hypertension and فقر دم صغير الكريات (microcytic anemia). It also has a relationship with قصور كلوي حاد (acute renal failure), reflecting its use in the management of severe kidney dysfunction. In clinical documentation, hemodialysis appears in a phenotypic data record identified by the string “病史:发现:时间点:^患者:名义型:PhenX”, indicating its relevance to patient history and phenotype collection. This interconnected knowledge highlights the role of hemodialysis in treating patients who present with chronic hypertension, microcytic anemia, and acute renal failure within the context of renal replacement therapy.

In addition to these acute complications, hemodialysis is clinically associated with chronic hypertension (hypertension chronic) and فقر دم صغير الكريات (microcytic anemia), reflecting common comorbidities observed in patients receiving this therapy. Hemodialysis also has a relationship with قصور كلوي حاد (acute renal failure), underscoring its use in the setting of severe kidney dysfunction and its potential to be involved in the clinical course of rapid renal decline. Documentation of patient encounters involving hemodialysis may include detailed historical data such as 病史:发现:时间点:^患者:名义型:PhenX, which captures the timing and nature of findings in a standardized PhenX format.
